CWE-345 - Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity

threat.title: Card Testing and Carding Attacks - Batch Payment Validation
threat.description: Card testing (carding) is a fraudulent technique where attackers use automated scripts and tools to validate stolen or generated credit card numbers through rapid-fire small transactions (typically under $1). Attackers upload lists of potential card numbers to payment systems or e-commerce platforms and process multiple verification transactions to identify which cards are active and valid. These validated card numbers are then sold on dark markets or used for larger fraudulent transactions. The 2024 Global Payment Report indicates carding attacks cost e-commerce merchants $28 billion annually in fraud losses and chargeback fees. Target, Home Depot, and Equifax breaches exemplified how compromised card data feeds carding ecosystems. Modern carding attacks bypass CAPTCHAs using computer vision AI, automate account creation at scale, and rotate through proxy networks to evade detection. Payment processors report 500-1000% increases in carding attempt volumes during holiday shopping seasons.

versions_affected: All payment processors without rate limiting
All e-commerce platforms without bot detection
All payment gateways without velocity checks
technology.ecosystem: Stripe, Square, PayPal, AWS Payment Cryptography, Adyen, Authorize.net, 2Checkout, Payment.js, Braintree, Worldpay

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker obtains 10,000-100,000 credit card numbers from data breach, dark web marketplace, or skimming operation
2. Sets up bot network using residential proxies to mask request origins and distribute traffic across multiple ISPs
3. Automates CAPTCHA solving using AI-powered services (e.g., 2captcha, CapMonster) to bypass bot detection
4. Creates fake e-commerce accounts programmatically using email automation and SMS verification services
5. Launches batched payment verification attempts: processes $0.01-$2.00 test transactions for each card in the list
6. Payment processor returns transaction responses revealing which card numbers are valid (approved/declined messages leaked)
7. Attacker correlates responses with card numbers and builds list of ~15-30% valid cards from original dataset
8. Sells validated card database on dark markets (CVV, expiry, holder name) for $1-10 per card
9. Buyers use validated cards for high-value fraud: $500-$2000 purchases, account takeovers, identity theft
10. Merchants face massive chargeback fees ($15-$100 per invalid transaction), customer disputes, and reputation damage
11. Payment processors flag high-velocity test transactions but attackers adapt by throttling requests, rotating IP addresses, and distributing testing across time periods to evade detection thresholds
prerequisites: Access to stolen credit card database with PAN, expiry date, and optional CVV
Bot automation framework (Selenium, Playwright, or custom HTTP client)
Residential proxy service to hide attacker IP and distribute requests across geography
CAPTCHA solving service (2Captcha, CapMonster, or proprietary AI solver)
Disposable email and SMS services for account creation automation
Vulnerability in payment processor or merchant platform: no rate limiting, insufficient velocity checks, or response leakage
Access to payment testing endpoints without proper authentication or transaction logging

cve_references.description: Card testing vulnerability in Stripe Elements checkout - insufficient rate limiting on charge endpoint allowed batch verification of 50,000+ cards in single session
WooCommerce payment gateway - transaction response messages leaked card validation status allowing attackers to determine which cards were valid through error message analysis
Square payment API missing velocity checks on test transaction endpoint - allowed attacker to validate 100,000 card numbers in 48 hours without rate limiting
Shopify payment form - no CSRF protection on card verification endpoint allowed attackers to inject JavaScript and intercept card data during form submission
PayPal API - insufficient authentication on payment verification endpoint allowed unauthenticated users to check card validity status

real_world_breaches.incident: Saks Fifth Avenue and Lord & Taylor Carding Attack (2024)
real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers used stolen card data to verify 500,000+ credit cards through payment systems over 6 months. Estimated 180,000 valid cards from initial dataset. Fraudsters then used validated cards for $50M+ in unauthorized purchases and account takeovers. Customers experienced identity theft, account freezes, and credit damage.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Payment processor insufficient rate limiting, no real-time fraud detection for small test transactions, transaction response messages leaked card status

real_world_breaches.impact: 40 million credit card numbers stolen in November 2013. Attackers used data to conduct massive carding campaign validating cards at Target partners. Validated cards used for $1.5B+ in fraud over 6 months. Target paid $18.5M settlement and implemented chip card technology.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of network segmentation in payment processing, no velocity detection on test transactions, insider threat from HVAC vendor compromise

real_world_breaches.impact: 56 million payment cards compromised via malware in point-of-sale systems. Fraudsters validated cards through testing platforms. $40M+ in fraud losses from validated card usage. Merchants reported 3000% increase in chargeback rates for 2 years post-breach.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: POS malware injection, inadequate payment processor monitoring, no real-time card testing detection, ineffective fraud rules

real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers injected malware into BA payment processing JavaScript code over 2 years, harvesting 429,612 passenger payment card details. Criminals used data for carding attacks validating cards and committing $200M+ fraud. BA paid $230M GDPR fine and compensated customers.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: No Content Security Policy (CSP), JavaScript supply chain compromise, insufficient payment data segregation, no PCI-DSS compliance monitoring

real_world_breaches.impact: 147 million social security numbers and 209 million credit card records exposed. Created largest carding database in history. Estimated $500M+ in fraud losses from validated cards. Attackers combined carding with identity theft for account takeovers.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability (CVE-2017-5645), no network segmentation, inadequate intrusion detection for data exfiltration

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Payment processor insufficient rate limiting allowed attacker to validate 1M+ card numbers in single campaign. No velocity checks on charge endpoint. Fixed with sliding window rate limiting and advanced fraud detection.
E-commerce payment form lacked velocity checks and allowed batch card testing through automated form submission. Added real-time monitoring and per-IP transaction limits.

Payment gateway transaction response messages leaked card validity through error code patterns. Attackers determined valid cards by analyzing success/failure responses. Standardized responses to prevent information leakage.

security_research.title: Carding: The Global Crisis of Credit Card Testing and Fraud
security_research.key_finding: Carding attacks increased 340% year-over-year in 2023. Attackers successfully validate 15-45% of stolen card datasets through merchant payment systems. Average validation time reduced from 3 months to 2 weeks due to improved automation and AI-powered CAPTCHA solving.

security_research.title: Bot Detection Bypass Techniques in Payment Systems
security_research.key_finding: Attackers use 5 primary techniques to bypass bot detection: residential proxy rotation, request throttling to stay under velocity limits, JavaScript rendering farms to mimic human behavior, distributed testing across time zones, and AI-powered CAPTCHA solving at 95%+ accuracy.

security_research.title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI-DSS) 4.0: Requirement 12.6 Card Testing Prevention
security_research.key_finding: PCI-DSS 4.0 mandates velocity checks, rate limiting, real-time fraud monitoring, and anomaly detection for payment transactions. Organizations must implement multi-factor authentication and strong encryption for payment endpoints.

security_research.title: Machine Learning for Fraud Detection in Real-Time Payment Systems
security_research.key_finding: ML-based fraud detection can identify 99.2% of carding attacks within first 5 test transactions using behavioral analysis, network analysis, and device fingerprinting. Manual review rate reduced from 10% to 2% while maintaining 99.8% legitimate transaction approval.

mitigations.title: Implement Advanced Rate Limiting and Velocity Checks
mitigations.description: Deploy sliding window rate limiting on payment endpoints with strict velocity checks for transaction attempts. Implement per-IP, per-customer, per-card, and per-device rate limits at multiple layers. Use adaptive rate limiting that tightens thresholds based on detected attack patterns. Monitor for suspicious patterns: multiple failed transactions followed by successes, transactions under $2, geographic velocity violations (impossible travel), and batch processing behavior.

code_example.code:
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_limiter import Limiter
from flask_limiter.util import get_remote_address
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import redis
import json
from typing import Tuple, Dict
import logging

app = Flask(__name__)
redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0)
limiter = Limiter(
    app=app,
    key_func=get_remote_address,
    default_limits=["200 per day", "50 per hour"],
    storage_uri="redis://localhost:6379"
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class VelocityChecker:
    """
    Advanced velocity checking for carding attack detection
    """
    
    def __init__(self, redis_client):
        self.redis = redis_client
        # Rate limit thresholds
        self.thresholds = {
            'per_ip_per_minute': 5,           # Max 5 transactions per IP per minute
            'per_ip_per_hour': 50,             # Max 50 transactions per IP per hour
            'per_card_per_day': 3,             # Max 3 transactions per card per day
            'per_customer_per_hour': 10,       # Max 10 transactions per customer per hour
            'low_amount_threshold': 2.0,       # Amounts < $2 are test transactions
            'low_amount_max_per_hour': 5,      # Max 5 test transactions per hour
            'high_velocity_threshold': 20      # Suspicious if > 20 txns in 1 hour
        }
    
    def check_ip_velocity(self, ip_address: str) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Check transaction velocity from single IP address
        Returns: (is_allowed, violation_details)
        """
        key_minute = f"ip_velocity:minute:{ip_address}"
        key_hour = f"ip_velocity:hour:{ip_address}"
        
        # Get current transaction counts
        txn_minute = int(self.redis.get(key_minute) or 0)
        txn_hour = int(self.redis.get(key_hour) or 0)
        
        # Check thresholds
        violations = []
        if txn_minute >= self.thresholds['per_ip_per_minute']:
            violations.append(f"IP exceeded {self.thresholds['per_ip_per_minute']} txns/minute")
        if txn_hour >= self.thresholds['per_ip_per_hour']:
            violations.append(f"IP exceeded {self.thresholds['per_ip_per_hour']} txns/hour")
        
        # Increment counters with TTL
        self.redis.incr(key_minute)
        self.redis.expire(key_minute, 60)
        self.redis.incr(key_hour)
        self.redis.expire(key_hour, 3600)
        
        allowed = len(violations) == 0
        return allowed, {
            'ip': ip_address,
            'txn_minute': txn_minute + 1,
            'txn_hour': txn_hour + 1,
            'violations': violations
        }
    
    def check_card_velocity(self, card_hash: str, amount: float) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Check transaction velocity for specific card
        Detects test transactions and card testing attacks
        """
        key_day = f"card_velocity:day:{card_hash}"
        key_hour_low = f"card_velocity:hour_low:{card_hash}"
        
        txn_day = int(self.redis.get(key_day) or 0)
        txn_low_hour = int(self.redis.get(key_hour_low) or 0)
        
        violations = []
        
        # Check daily limit (max 3 transactions per card per day)
        if txn_day >= self.thresholds['per_card_per_day']:
            violations.append(f"Card exceeded {self.thresholds['per_card_per_day']} txns/day")
        
        # Check low-amount transaction pattern (carding indicator)
        if amount < self.thresholds['low_amount_threshold']:
            if txn_low_hour >= self.thresholds['low_amount_max_per_hour']:
                violations.append(f"Card exceeded {self.thresholds['low_amount_max_per_hour']} test txns/hour")
            self.redis.incr(key_hour_low)
            self.redis.expire(key_hour_low, 3600)
        
        # Increment daily counter
        self.redis.incr(key_day)
        self.redis.expire(key_day, 86400)
        
        allowed = len(violations) == 0
        return allowed, {
            'card_hash': card_hash,
            'txn_day': txn_day + 1,
            'amount': amount,
            'violations': violations
        }
    
    def check_geographic_velocity(self, customer_id: str, ip_address: str, 
                                  timestamp: datetime) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Detect impossible travel - transactions from geographically distant locations
        within impossible time frame
        """
        key_location = f"geo_velocity:{customer_id}:last_location"
        
        last_location = self.redis.get(key_location)
        if not last_location:
            self.redis.setex(key_location, 3600, ip_address)
            return True, {'status': 'first_transaction'}
        
        last_location = last_location.decode('utf-8')
        
        # Simple geo-IP lookup (in production, use MaxMind GeoIP2)
        # This is simplified - in reality use proper geolocation API
        if last_location != ip_address:
            violations = [f"Transaction from different IP: {last_location} -> {ip_address}"]
            return False, {
                'customer_id': customer_id,
                'last_ip': last_location,
                'current_ip': ip_address,
                'violations': violations
            }
        
        return True, {'status': 'same_location'}
    
    def check_customer_velocity(self, customer_id: str) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Check transaction velocity per customer account
        Detects account takeover and abuse patterns
        """
        key_hour = f"customer_velocity:hour:{customer_id}"
        
        txn_hour = int(self.redis.get(key_hour) or 0)
        
        violations = []
        if txn_hour >= self.thresholds['per_customer_per_hour']:
            violations.append(f"Customer exceeded {self.thresholds['per_customer_per_hour']} txns/hour")
        
        self.redis.incr(key_hour)
        self.redis.expire(key_hour, 3600)
        
        allowed = len(violations) == 0
        return allowed, {
            'customer_id': customer_id,
            'txn_hour': txn_hour + 1,
            'violations': violations
        }

# Initialize velocity checker
velocity_checker = VelocityChecker(redis_client)

@app.route('/api/charge', methods=['POST'])
@limiter.limit("5 per minute")  # Global rate limit
def charge():
    """
    Process payment with velocity checking
    """
    try:
        data = request.get_json()
        
        # Extract payment data
        amount = float(data.get('amount', 0))
        card_token = data.get('card_token')
        customer_id = data.get('customer_id')
        ip_address = request.remote_addr
        
        # Validate inputs
        if not all([amount, card_token, customer_id]):
            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required fields'}), 400
        
        if amount <= 0:
            return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid amount'}), 400
        
        # Hash card token for privacy
        import hashlib
        card_hash = hashlib.sha256(card_token.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
        
        # Check velocity from multiple angles
        timestamp = datetime.utcnow()
        
        # 1. IP velocity
        ip_allowed, ip_details = velocity_checker.check_ip_velocity(ip_address)
        if not ip_allowed:
            logger.warning(f"IP velocity violation: {ip_details}")
            return jsonify({
                'error': 'Too many transactions from your IP',
                'code': 'VELOCITY_IP_EXCEEDED',
                'details': ip_details
            }), 429
        
        # 2. Card velocity
        card_allowed, card_details = velocity_checker.check_card_velocity(card_hash, amount)
        if not card_allowed:
            logger.warning(f"Card velocity violation: {card_details}")
            return jsonify({
                'error': 'Too many transactions for this card',
                'code': 'VELOCITY_CARD_EXCEEDED',
                'details': card_details
            }), 429
        
        # 3. Customer velocity
        customer_allowed, customer_details = velocity_checker.check_customer_velocity(customer_id)
        if not customer_allowed:
            logger.warning(f"Customer velocity violation: {customer_details}")
            return jsonify({
                'error': 'Too many transactions for this account',
                'code': 'VELOCITY_CUSTOMER_EXCEEDED',
                'details': customer_details
            }), 429
        
        # 4. Geographic velocity
        geo_allowed, geo_details = velocity_checker.check_geographic_velocity(
            customer_id, ip_address, timestamp
        )
        if not geo_allowed:
            logger.warning(f"Geographic velocity violation: {geo_details}")
            return jsonify({
                'error': 'Suspicious geographic velocity detected',
                'code': 'VELOCITY_GEO_EXCEEDED',
                'details': geo_details
            }), 429
        
        # Velocity checks passed - process charge
        logger.info(f"Processing charge: customer={customer_id}, amount=${amount}, ip={ip_address}")
        
        # In production, call actual payment processor
        charge_result = {
            'success': True,
            'transaction_id': 'txn_123456',
            'amount': amount,
            'status': 'succeeded'
        }
        
        return jsonify(charge_result), 200
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Charge processing error: {str(e)}")
        return jsonify({'error': 'Internal server error'}), 500

@app.route('/health', methods=['GET'])
def health():
    """Health check endpoint"""
    return jsonify({'status': 'healthy'}), 200

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=False, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000)

tools.name: Redis
tools.type: In-Memory Data Store for Rate Limit Tracking

iso27001_controls: A.13.1.1 - Information Transfer Policies and Procedures
nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
PR.AC-3 - Access is enforced with and without data loss prevention
validation_steps: Verify rate limiting is enforced: test > 5 requests/minute from single IP returns 429 status
Confirm card velocity tracking: max 3 transactions per card per day
Check low-amount transaction detection: flag if > 5 transactions < $2/hour from same card
Validate geographic velocity: detect impossible travel (e.g., New York to Tokyo in 5 minutes)
Test customer velocity: limit to 10 transactions per customer per hour
Verify rate limit metrics are logged and monitored in SIEM
Confirm blocked requests trigger alerts for fraud team review

mitigations.title: Deploy Real-Time Machine Learning Fraud Detection
mitigations.description: Implement ML-based fraud detection models that analyze transaction patterns in real-time. Use behavioral biometrics, device fingerprinting, network analysis, and transaction anomaly detection. Models should detect carding attacks within 5 transactions by identifying test transaction patterns, velocity spikes, and bot behavior. Integrate with payment processors' native fraud detection (Stripe Radar, Adyen Fraud Prevention, PayPal Seller Protection). Use ensemble models combining rule-based detection and neural networks for 99%+ attack detection rates.

code_example.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, LabelEncoder
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier, IsolationForest
import xgboost as xgb
from tensorflow import keras
from tensorflow.keras import layers
import joblib
import logging
from typing import Tuple, Dict, List
import redis
import hashlib

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class CardingFraudDetector:
    """
    ML-based fraud detection specifically tuned for carding attacks
    """
    
    def __init__(self, redis_client):
        self.redis = redis_client
        self.xgb_model = None
        self.neural_network = None
        self.isolation_forest = None
        self.scaler = StandardScaler()
        self.risk_threshold = 0.7  # 70% risk score triggers review
        
        # Load or initialize models
        self._initialize_models()
    
    def _initialize_models(self):
        """
        Initialize ML models for fraud detection
        In production, load pre-trained models from model registry
        """
        # XGBoost classifier for carding pattern detection
        self.xgb_model = xgb.XGBClassifier(
            n_estimators=200,
            max_depth=8,
            learning_rate=0.1,
            subsample=0.8,
            colsample_bytree=0.8,
            objective='binary:logistic',
            random_state=42
        )
        
        # Isolation Forest for anomaly detection
        self.isolation_forest = IsolationForest(
            contamination=0.05,  # Assume 5% of transactions are anomalous
            random_state=42
        )
        
        # Neural network for behavioral analysis
        self.neural_network = self._build_neural_network()
    
    def _build_neural_network(self):
        """
        Build deep neural network for transaction classification
        """
        model = keras.Sequential([
            layers.Input(shape=(32,)),  # 32 input features
            layers.Dense(128, activation='relu', kernel_regularizer=keras.regularizers.l2(0.001)),
            layers.Dropout(0.3),
            layers.Dense(64, activation='relu', kernel_regularizer=keras.regularizers.l2(0.001)),
            layers.Dropout(0.3),
            layers.Dense(32, activation='relu'),
            layers.Dropout(0.2),
            layers.Dense(16, activation='relu'),
            layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid')  # Binary classification: fraud/legitimate
        ])
        
        model.compile(
            optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.001),
            loss='binary_crossentropy',
            metrics=['accuracy', keras.metrics.AUC()]
        )
        
        return model
    
    def extract_features(self, transaction: Dict) -> np.ndarray:
        """
        Extract 32 features from transaction for ML models
        Features target carding attack patterns
        """
        features = []
        
        # 1. Transaction amount features
        amount = transaction.get('amount', 0)
        features.append(amount)  # Absolute amount
        features.append(1 if amount < 2.0 else 0)  # Test transaction flag
        features.append(1 if amount < 5.0 else 0)  # Low amount flag
        features.append(amount % 1 == 0)  # Is round number
        
        # 2. Temporal features
        timestamp = datetime.fromisoformat(transaction.get('timestamp', ''))
        features.append(timestamp.hour)  # Hour of day
        features.append(timestamp.weekday())  # Day of week
        features.append(int(timestamp.timestamp()) % 86400)  # Seconds since midnight
        
        # 3. Velocity features (from Redis)
        customer_id = transaction.get('customer_id')
        card_hash = hashlib.sha256(
            transaction.get('card_token', '').encode()
        ).hexdigest()[:16]
        
        # Get transaction counts from Redis
        card_txn_hour = int(self.redis.get(f"card_velocity:hour:{card_hash}") or 0)
        card_txn_day = int(self.redis.get(f"card_velocity:day:{card_hash}") or 0)
        customer_txn_hour = int(self.redis.get(f"customer_velocity:hour:{customer_id}") or 0)
        ip_txn_minute = int(self.redis.get(f"ip_velocity:minute:{transaction.get('ip_address')}") or 0)
        
        features.append(card_txn_hour)      # Transactions per card per hour
        features.append(card_txn_day)       # Transactions per card per day
        features.append(customer_txn_hour)  # Transactions per customer per hour
        features.append(ip_txn_minute)      # Transactions per IP per minute
        
        # 4. Device fingerprinting features
        device_id = transaction.get('device_id', 'unknown')
        features.append(hash(device_id) % 100)  # Device ID hash
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('user_agent', '') == '' else 0)  # Missing user agent
        features.append(1 if 'bot' in transaction.get('user_agent', '').lower() else 0)  # Bot detection
        
        # 5. Location features
        ip_address = transaction.get('ip_address', '')
        features.append(hash(ip_address) % 1000)  # IP hash
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('country_code') == 'US' else 0)  # US flag
        
        # 6. Card features
        card_country = transaction.get('card_country', 'US')
        features.append(1 if card_country == 'US' else 0)  # Card in US
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('card_brand', '') == '' else 0)  # Missing card brand
        
        # 7. Previous transaction patterns
        # Get customer's last 5 transactions from Redis
        last_txns = self.redis.lrange(f"customer_history:{customer_id}", 0, 4)
        features.append(len(last_txns))  # Number of previous transactions
        
        # Average amount from previous transactions
        if last_txns:
            amounts = [float(t.split(':')[1]) for t in [x.decode() for x in last_txns]]
            features.append(np.mean(amounts))  # Average transaction amount
            features.append(np.std(amounts))   # Std deviation of amounts
        else:
            features.append(0)
            features.append(0)
        
        # 8. Authentication factors
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('three_d_secure', False) else 0)  # 3DS enabled
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('cvv_verified', False) else 0)    # CVV verified
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('address_verified', False) else 0)  # AVS passed
        
        # 9. Account features
        account_age_days = (datetime.utcnow() - 
                          datetime.fromisoformat(transaction.get('account_created', ''))).days
        features.append(account_age_days)  # Account age in days
        features.append(1 if account_age_days < 7 else 0)  # New account flag
        features.append(1 if account_age_days < 1 else 0)  # Very new account flag
        
        # 10. Behavioral features
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('is_repeat_customer', False) else 0)  # Repeat customer
        features.append(1 if transaction.get('shipping_address') != transaction.get('billing_address') else 0)  # Address mismatch
        
        # Convert to numpy array and scale
        features_array = np.array(features, dtype=np.float32)
        
        # Ensure we have exactly 32 features
        if len(features_array) < 32:
            features_array = np.pad(features_array, (0, 32 - len(features_array)))
        elif len(features_array) > 32:
            features_array = features_array[:32]
        
        return features_array.reshape(1, -1)
    
    def detect_fraud(self, transaction: Dict) -> Tuple[float, Dict]:
        """
        Run all fraud detection models and return combined risk score
        Returns: (risk_score: 0.0-1.0, details: Dict)
        """
        details = {
            'transaction_id': transaction.get('id'),
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'models': {}
        }
        
        try:
            # Extract features
            features = self.extract_features(transaction)
            
            # 1. XGBoost classification
            xgb_proba = self.xgb_model.predict_proba(features)[0][1]
            details['models']['xgboost'] = {
                'fraud_probability': float(xgb_proba),
                'weight': 0.4
            }
            
            # 2. Neural network classification
            nn_proba = self.neural_network.predict(features, verbose=0)[0][0]
            details['models']['neural_network'] = {
                'fraud_probability': float(nn_proba),
                'weight': 0.4
            }
            
            # 3. Isolation Forest anomaly detection
            anomaly_score = self.isolation_forest.decision_function(features)[0]
            anomaly_proba = 1 / (1 + np.exp(-anomaly_score))  # Convert to probability
            details['models']['isolation_forest'] = {
                'anomaly_probability': float(anomaly_proba),
                'weight': 0.2
            }
            
            # 4. Rule-based detection (carding-specific patterns)
            rule_score = self._apply_detection_rules(transaction)
            details['models']['rule_based'] = {
                'fraud_probability': float(rule_score),
                'weight': 0.3
            }
            
            # 5. Calculate ensemble risk score (weighted average)
            total_weight = 0.4 + 0.4 + 0.2 + 0.3  # Sum of weights
            risk_score = (
                (xgb_proba * 0.4) +
                (nn_proba * 0.4) +
                (anomaly_proba * 0.2) +
                (rule_score * 0.3)
            ) / total_weight
            
            details['risk_score'] = float(risk_score)
            details['action'] = self._get_action_for_risk(risk_score)
            
            return float(risk_score), details
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Fraud detection error: {str(e)}")
            # Default to conservative risk score on error
            return 0.5, {'error': str(e)}
    
    def _apply_detection_rules(self, transaction: Dict) -> float:
        """
        Rule-based detection for known carding attack patterns
        """
        risk_score = 0.0
        amount = transaction.get('amount', 0)
        
        # Rule 1: Test transaction amount ($0.01 - $2.00)
        if 0 < amount < 2:
            risk_score += 0.3
        
        # Rule 2: High velocity from new account
        if transaction.get('account_age_days', 100) < 7:
            risk_score += 0.2
        
        # Rule 3: Multiple test transactions in short time
        card_hash = hashlib.sha256(
            transaction.get('card_token', '').encode()
        ).hexdigest()[:16]
        card_txn_hour = int(self.redis.get(f"card_velocity:hour:{card_hash}") or 0)
        if card_txn_hour > 3:
            risk_score += 0.25
        
        # Rule 4: IP from proxy/VPN (simplified - use MaxMind in production)
        if transaction.get('is_proxy', False) or transaction.get('is_vpn', False):
            risk_score += 0.15
        
        # Rule 5: Missing CVV or failed CVV verification
        if not transaction.get('cvv_verified', False):
            risk_score += 0.1
        
        # Rule 6: Mismatched shipping and billing address
        if transaction.get('shipping_address') != transaction.get('billing_address'):
            risk_score += 0.1
        
        # Normalize to 0-1 range
        return min(risk_score, 1.0)
    
    def _get_action_for_risk(self, risk_score: float) -> str:
        """
        Determine action based on risk score
        """
        if risk_score >= 0.85:
            return 'block'  # Definitely fraud
        elif risk_score >= 0.7:
            return 'challenge'  # Request additional verification
        elif risk_score >= 0.5:
            return 'review'  # Manual review by fraud team
        else:
            return 'approve'  # Low risk, approve automatically

class FraudDetectionAPI:
    """
    API for fraud detection integration
    """
    
    def __init__(self, detector: CardingFraudDetector):
        self.detector = detector
    
    def check_transaction(self, transaction: Dict) -> Dict:
        """
        Check transaction for fraud and return decision
        """
        risk_score, details = self.detector.detect_fraud(transaction)
        
        decision = {
            'transaction_id': transaction.get('id'),
            'risk_score': risk_score,
            'risk_level': self._get_risk_level(risk_score),
            'recommended_action': details.get('action'),
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        }
        
        # Log for analysis
        logger.info(f"Fraud check: {decision}")
        
        return decision
    
    def _get_risk_level(self, risk_score: float) -> str:
        if risk_score >= 0.85:
            return 'critical'
        elif risk_score >= 0.7:
            return 'high'
        elif risk_score >= 0.5:
            return 'medium'
        else:
            return 'low'

# Integration example with Flask
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify

app = Flask(__name__)
redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0)
detector = CardingFraudDetector(redis_client)
api = FraudDetectionAPI(detector)

@app.route('/api/fraud-check', methods=['POST'])
def fraud_check():
    """
    Real-time fraud detection endpoint
    """
    try:
        transaction = request.get_json()
        decision = api.check_transaction(transaction)
        
        # Return decision to payment processor
        if decision['recommended_action'] == 'block':
            return jsonify({
                'approved': False,
                'reason': 'Fraudulent transaction detected',
                'risk_score': decision['risk_score']
            }), 403
        elif decision['recommended_action'] == 'challenge':
            return jsonify({
                'approved': True,
                'challenge_required': True,
                'challenge_type': '3d_secure',
                'risk_score': decision['risk_score']
            }), 200
        else:
            return jsonify({
                'approved': True,
                'risk_score': decision['risk_score']
            }), 200
            
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error in fraud check: {str(e)}")
        return jsonify({'error': 'Fraud check failed'}), 500

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=False, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000)

Check model retrains weekly on new fraud data to adapt to attacks

Test ensemble model achieves 99%+ AUC-ROC on validation dataset
Monitor model drift and performance metrics in production

mitigations.title: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication and Strong Customer Authentication
mitigations.description: Enforce 3D Secure 2.0 (3DS2), cryptogram verification, and Risk-Based Authentication (RBA) for high-risk transactions. Implement behavioral biometrics for frictionless authentication. Require additional verification (SMS OTP, TOTP, push notification) for transactions flagged as high-risk by fraud detection. Use FIDO2/WebAuthn for premium accounts. Implement device fingerprinting and continuous authentication throughout transaction lifecycle.

PR.AC-2 - Physical access is managed, monitored, and controlled
validation_steps: Test 3DS2 triggers for transactions with risk score >= 65%
Verify SMS OTP expires after 5 minutes and can only be used once
Confirm TOTP generation creates valid authenticator codes
Test device fingerprint detects anomalous access patterns
Validate behavioral biometrics block bot-like patterns
Ensure all MFA codes use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks
Monitor false rejection rate to ensure good customer experience

mitigations.title: Monitor and Block Proxy/VPN Traffic and Bot Activity
mitigations.description: Implement geolocation and proxy detection to identify attacker infrastructure. Block transactions from data center IP ranges, residential proxies, and VPN services known for abuse. Monitor for bot behavior: headless browser signatures, automated form filling, unusual user-agent patterns, and JavaScript execution failures. Integrate with threat intelligence feeds (MaxMind, Abuseipdb) to dynamically block malicious IPs. Implement CAPTCHA and bot challenge systems that resist automation (invisible challenges for human users).

code_example.framework: Flask with MaxMind GeoIP2 and hCaptcha
code_example.code:
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
import geoip2.database
import requests
import logging
import redis
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Tuple, Dict
import hashlib

app = Flask(__name__)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0)

class ProxyAndBotDetector:
    """
    Detect proxy/VPN usage and bot activity patterns
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        # MaxMind GeoIP2 database
        try:
            self.geoip_reader = geoip2.database.Reader('/opt/maxmind/GeoLite2-City.mmdb')
            self.isp_reader = geoip2.database.Reader('/opt/maxmind/GeoIP2-ISP.mmdb')
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"MaxMind database error: {e}")
            self.geoip_reader = None
            self.isp_reader = None
        
        # Known proxy/VPN data center ASNs
        self.known_proxy_asns = {
            '8452',   # TeData (Egyptian ISP, high abuse)
            '16509',  # Amazon AWS
            '14061',  # DigitalOcean
            '14061',  # Linode
            '15169',  # Google Cloud
            '16509',  # Azure
            '394536', # Alibaba Cloud
        }
        
        # Suspicious user agent patterns (bot detection)
        self.bot_patterns = [
            'bot',
            'crawler',
            'spider',
            'headless',
            'phantom',
            'selenium',
            'puppeteer',
            'playwright',
            'watir',
            'curl',
            'wget',
            'scrapy'
        ]
        
        # Threat intelligence - AbuseIPDB API
        self.abuseipdb_api_key = 'YOUR_ABUSEIPDB_API_KEY'
        self.threat_cache_ttl = 3600  # 1 hour
    
    def detect_proxy_vpn(self, ip_address: str) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Detect if IP is from proxy, VPN, or data center
        Returns: (is_proxy_vpn, details)
        """
        details = {
            'ip_address': ip_address,
            'is_proxy_vpn': False,
            'indicators': []
        }
        
        try:
            # Check GeoIP database
            if self.geoip_reader:
                city_response = self.geoip_reader.city(ip_address)
                isp_response = self.isp_reader.isp(ip_address)
                
                details['location'] = {
                    'country': city_response.country.iso_code,
                    'city': city_response.city.name,
                    'latitude': city_response.location.latitude,
                    'longitude': city_response.location.longitude
                }
                details['isp'] = isp_response.isp_name
                details['asn'] = isp_response.autonomous_system_number
                
                # Check if ASN is known proxy/VPN
                if str(isp_response.autonomous_system_number) in self.known_proxy_asns:
                    details['is_proxy_vpn'] = True
                    details['indicators'].append(f"Known proxy ASN: {isp_response.autonomous_system_number}")
            
            # Query AbuseIPDB for reputation
            abuse_info = self._query_abuseipdb(ip_address)
            if abuse_info and abuse_info.get('abuseConfidenceScore', 0) > 50:
                details['is_proxy_vpn'] = True
                details['indicators'].append(f"High abuse score: {abuse_info['abuseConfidenceScore']}")
                details['abuse_reports'] = abuse_info.get('totalReports', 0)
            
            # Check Redis cache for known proxies
            cached_result = redis_client.get(f"proxy_check:{ip_address}")
            if cached_result:
                cached = json.loads(cached_result)
                if cached.get('is_proxy_vpn'):
                    details['is_proxy_vpn'] = True
                    details['indicators'].append("Cached as proxy")
            
            return details['is_proxy_vpn'], details
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Proxy detection error: {e}")
            return False, details
    
    def _query_abuseipdb(self, ip_address: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Query AbuseIPDB for IP reputation
        """
        try:
            # Check cache first
            cache_key = f"abuseipdb:{ip_address}"
            cached = redis_client.get(cache_key)
            if cached:
                return json.loads(cached)
            
            # Query API
            headers = {
                'Key': self.abuseipdb_api_key,
                'Accept': 'application/json'
            }
            params = {
                'ipAddress': ip_address,
                'maxAgeInDays': 30,
                'verbose': ''
            }
            
            response = requests.get(
                'https://api.abuseipdb.com/api/v2/check',
                headers=headers,
                params=params,
                timeout=5
            )
            
            if response.status_code == 200:
                data = response.json()['data']
                # Cache result
                redis_client.setex(cache_key, self.threat_cache_ttl, json.dumps(data))
                return data
            
            return None
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"AbuseIPDB query error: {e}")
            return None
    
    def detect_bot_activity(self, user_agent: str, request_headers: Dict) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Detect bot activity through user-agent, headers, and behavior
        Returns: (is_bot, details)
        """
        details = {
            'is_bot': False,
            'indicators': [],
            'score': 0.0
        }
        
        user_agent_lower = user_agent.lower()
        
        # Check 1: Suspicious user agent patterns
        for pattern in self.bot_patterns:
            if pattern in user_agent_lower:
                details['is_bot'] = True
                details['indicators'].append(f"Bot keyword detected: {pattern}")
                details['score'] += 0.3
        
        # Check 2: Missing or unusual headers
        if not request_headers.get('User-Agent'):
            details['indicators'].append("Missing User-Agent header")
            details['score'] += 0.2
        
        if not request_headers.get('Accept-Language'):
            details['indicators'].append("Missing Accept-Language header")
            details['score'] += 0.15
        
        if not request_headers.get('Accept-Encoding'):
            details['indicators'].append("Missing Accept-Encoding header")
            details['score'] += 0.15
        
        # Check 3: Headless browser signatures
        if 'HeadlessChrome' in user_agent or 'PhantomJS' in user_agent:
            details['is_bot'] = True
            details['indicators'].append("Headless browser detected")
            details['score'] += 0.4
        
        # Check 4: Chrome WebDriver
        if 'Chrome' in user_agent and request_headers.get('Chrome-Lighthouse'):
            details['is_bot'] = True
            details['indicators'].append("WebDriver signature detected")
            details['score'] += 0.4
        
        # Check 5: Mobile inconsistencies
        is_mobile = 'Mobile' in user_agent or 'Android' in user_agent
        if is_mobile and not request_headers.get('Device-Memory'):
            details['indicators'].append("Mobile claims without device memory")
            details['score'] += 0.1
        
        return details['score'] >= 0.5, details
    
    def detect_geographic_impossibilities(self, 
                                        previous_location: Dict,
                                        current_location: Dict,
                                        time_delta_seconds: int) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Detect impossible geographic movement patterns
        """
        details = {
            'is_impossible_travel': False,
            'indicators': []
        }
        
        if not previous_location or not current_location:
            return False, details
        
        # Calculate great circle distance between two points
        from math import radians, cos, sin, asin, sqrt
        
        lat1 = radians(previous_location['latitude'])
        lon1 = radians(previous_location['longitude'])
        lat2 = radians(current_location['latitude'])
        lon2 = radians(current_location['longitude'])
        
        dlat = lat2 - lat1
        dlon = lon2 - lon1
        a = sin(dlat/2)**2 + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin(dlon/2)**2
        c = 2 * asin(sqrt(a))
        r = 6371  # Earth radius in kilometers
        distance_km = c * r
        
        # Maximum speed: Mach 1 (~1234 km/h)
        max_possible_km = (time_delta_seconds / 3600) * 1234
        
        if distance_km > max_possible_km:
            details['is_impossible_travel'] = True
            details['indicators'].append(
                f"Impossible travel: {distance_km:.1f} km in {time_delta_seconds} seconds"
            )
        
        return details['is_impossible_travel'], details

class BotChallenge:
    """
    Implement bot challenges and CAPTCHA
    """
    
    def __init__(self, hcaptcha_secret: str):
        self.hcaptcha_secret = hcaptcha_secret
    
    def verify_hcaptcha(self, hcaptcha_token: str) -> Tuple[bool, Dict]:
        """
        Verify hCaptcha response
        """
        try:
            response = requests.post(
                'https://hcaptcha.com/siteverify',
                data={
                    'secret': self.hcaptcha_secret,
                    'response': hcaptcha_token
                },
                timeout=5
            )
            
            result = response.json()
            return result.get('success', False), result
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"CAPTCHA verification error: {e}")
            return False, {'error': str(e)}
    
    def create_challenge(self, challenge_type: str = 'hcaptcha') -> Dict:
        """
        Create bot challenge (CAPTCHA, proof of work, etc)
        """
        if challenge_type == 'hcaptcha':
            return {
                'type': 'hcaptcha',
                'sitekey': 'YOUR_HCAPTCHA_SITEKEY',
                'endpoint': '/api/verify-captcha'
            }
        elif challenge_type == 'proof_of_work':
            # Require client to solve crypto puzzle
            import secrets
            difficulty = 24
            nonce = secrets.token_hex(16)
            return {
                'type': 'proof_of_work',
                'nonce': nonce,
                'difficulty': difficulty,
                'endpoint': '/api/verify-pow'
            }
        else:
            return {}

# Initialize detectors
proxy_detector = ProxyAndBotDetector()
bot_challenge = BotChallenge(hcaptcha_secret='YOUR_HCAPTCHA_SECRET')

@app.route('/api/charge', methods=['POST'])
def charge():
    """
    Charge endpoint with bot and proxy detection
    """
    try:
        data = request.get_json()
        ip_address = request.remote_addr
        user_agent = request.headers.get('User-Agent', '')
        
        # Detect proxy/VPN
        is_proxy, proxy_details = proxy_detector.detect_proxy_vpn(ip_address)
        if is_proxy:
            logger.warning(f"Proxy/VPN detected: {proxy_details}")
            return jsonify({
                'error': 'Transactions from proxies/VPNs are not allowed',
                'code': 'PROXY_DETECTED'
            }), 403
        
        # Detect bot activity
        is_bot, bot_details = proxy_detector.detect_bot_activity(
            user_agent,
            dict(request.headers)
        )
        
        if is_bot:
            logger.warning(f"Bot detected: {bot_details}")
            # Challenge bot
            challenge = bot_challenge.create_challenge('hcaptcha')
            return jsonify({
                'error': 'Suspicious activity detected. Please verify you are human.',
                'code': 'BOT_DETECTED',
                'challenge': challenge
            }), 429
        
        # Process charge if checks pass
        return jsonify({
            'success': True,
            'transaction_id': 'txn_123456'
        }), 200
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Charge error: {e}")
        return jsonify({'error': 'Internal server error'}), 500

@app.route('/api/verify-captcha', methods=['POST'])
def verify_captcha():
    """
    Verify CAPTCHA response
    """
    try:
        data = request.get_json()
        hcaptcha_token = data.get('token')
        
        success, result = bot_challenge.verify_hcaptcha(hcaptcha_token)
        
        if success:
            # Allow transaction after successful verification
            return jsonify({'verified': True}), 200
        else:
            return jsonify({
                'verified': False,
                'error': 'CAPTCHA verification failed'
            }), 400
            
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"CAPTCHA verification error: {e}")
        return jsonify({'error': 'Verification failed'}), 500

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=False, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000)

Monitor detection rules and update threat intelligence daily

indicators: 5-50 declined payment transactions followed by 1-3 successful transactions from same card/IP
Rapid sequence of $0.01-$2.00 transactions (test amounts) from single card
High-velocity transactions from data center IP addresses or proxy services
Headless browser signatures or Selenium/Playwright user agent strings
Multiple failed SMS verification attempts before successful transaction
Geographic velocity violations: transactions from distant locations within impossible timeframes
Account creation immediately followed by payment transactions
Batch creation of accounts using similar email patterns/phone numbers
Transactions from known carding hotspot countries with high abuse rates
Missing or suspicious HTTP headers (no User-Agent, Accept-Language, etc)

log_queries.query:
index=payment_logs sourcetype=stripe
(charge_status=failed OR charge_status=declined)
| stats count as failed_count by customer_id, card_token
| where failed_count >= 5
| append [search index=payment_logs sourcetype=stripe charge_status=succeeded customer_id=* card_token=* | stats count as success_count by customer_id, card_token | where success_count >= 1]
| stats values(failed_count) as failures, values(success_count) as successes by customer_id
| where failures > 5 AND successes >= 1
| eval risk="High - Carding Attack Pattern"
| table customer_id, failures, successes, risk

// Detect batch card testing pattern
Payment_Logs
| where Amount < 2.0  // Test transaction amounts
| where ChargeStatus in ('succeeded', 'failed')
| summarize FailedCount = countif(ChargeStatus == 'failed'), SuccessCount = countif(ChargeStatus == 'succeeded'), TxnCount = count() by CardLastFour, SourceIP
| where FailedCount >= 5 and SuccessCount >= 1
| extend Risk = "High - Batch Card Testing"
| project CardLastFour, SourceIP, FailedCount, SuccessCount, TxnCount, Risk

@payment.amount:[0 TO 2] @payment.status:"failed" OR "succeeded"
| stats count as total, count as failures by @payment.card_token, @network.client.ip
| filter failures > 5
| sort -failures
